page: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top tier. However, because the CPU allocation is shared, multi-core performance exhibits some resource-sharing tradeoffs, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falling into the average category. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the PassMark memory score and large-block write bandwidth both placing in the top tier.

This hardware profile is well-suited for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks, where it achieves strong to top-tier benchmark results. The shared CPU model offers a cost-effective solution for resource-dense workloads that require substantial memory and fast NVMe storage without requiring dedicated, continuous CPU execution.
description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD. While its shared CPU allocation leads to average multi-core benchmark results, it delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and exceptional memory write bandwidth. This resource-dense configuration provides a cost-effective option for memory-heavy and storage-reliant workloads. It is highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, Redis caching, and text-based LLM inference.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose server featuring 32 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1920 GB NVMe storage, offering top-tier single-core performance and cost-effective resource density.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A resource-dense virtual server combining thirty-two AMD EPYC cores, substantial memory, and fast NVMe storage for balanced, cost-effective workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory
Bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory write performance
Shared CPU allocation offering qualitative cost efficiency
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving
